rationale: The citing article is a broad conceptual editorial with a narrative historical review, so many prior JMR studies may be cited as examples rather than deeply engaged. The cited 2016 paper is clearly relevant to mitigation-type harms (contamination-based consumer avoidance) and could plausibly serve as mechanism/background support, but the only matched evidence here is a DOI reference entry with no in-text citation context. Given this sparse evidence, it cannot be safely dropped or confidently kept.
